text: I have been with Tmobile for over 14 years. Usually no problems and the customer service had always been great. In recent months I started a job at a company near Downtown Tampa. This location near the Buccaneers Stadium. Since i moved to work at this location the service has been awful, no internet and calls have been dropping. I have called multiple mullite times, with moment the tell me we will call you back with no call back or the issue has been resolve keep using it and call back if any problem persist. This last interaction they told me that looking at the address provided the area had been modernized that I should not be having the problem any further, but the reason I called Tmobile at the moment was BECAUSE I was having the issue STILL, but NO impossible I was still having a problem he had just told me the towers were modernized. Sadly at this point I'm waiting to pay off what I have left on my devices and sadly ill say goodbye to Tmobile, I need my service working and at this time its not. I wont be calling back to try and solve this issue, its simply a waste of time since even managers have not provided call backs.

I enjoy going to stores TO browse new merchandise and most of all Get exercise.. too many Large people get food taken to their cars as they sit there and wait for their ice cream, and candy bars, and Dr pepper... Walking is good for you.. Walmarts are big stores, can walk an easy mile or more shopping.. now T-mobile who have been Hacked before doesn't want me to walk into their stores and browse.  Instead I'm being pressured to join their Auto pay or be charged $8  to pay in person.. and actually have their employees do some work... time for us to look into another carrier

I have simplified the details of my experience somewhat for brevity.

I called to order an iPhone with a great offer on a trade in. The very friendly agent upsold me home internet and said he would throw in a free Samsung tablet, the only attached string being an additional $5 per month for the added cellular service. My cellular bill was a plan that cost $55 per month, and the internet service was to be $30. With a very generous trade in credit of $830, I was pleased with the deal. My total monthly bill, which includes a $20 per month protection plan and a senior discount was to be $97.

The agent sent me to docusign to sign the paperwork and almost immediately asked me what was taking me so long. I told him it was a lot of paperwork to read, and he urged, “don’t read, just sign.” That should have been a red flag.

Over the next few days, I began to get emails that did not look at all like what I had been told, so I started calling. Over the next few weeks, I talked to at least a dozen different agents, all of whom seemed very kind and helpful, and all of whom assured me that I should ignore the emails and my online account details. They said they were still processing my trade in and several promised further deep discounts on my plan to make up for the confusion. On one of these calls, I informed the agent that I had been approved for the federal Affordable Connectivity Program, which pays internet providers $30 toward the invoice for selected people. The agent kindly informed me that while T-Mobile does not honor this program, he would issue a monthly credit to my account for a minimum of $30 (probably more, he assured) to make up for it. Now I was expecting a bill of around $67 monthly, and I was very pleased.

Today marked 27 days from my purchase date, and I once again made one of my routine calls to hopefully get reassured once again that all of the promises made by a dozen agents were, in fact, true. 

It turns out nothing I had been told by any of them even remotely resembled the truth. My plan was not $55, it was $75. The tablet (a worthless 4G 32 gb and very slow and glitchy device whose operating system and included apps already maxed out its memory) was not free and required a $20 per month 5G contract. That’s right. They required me to get a 5G plan for a device that can’t even access it! My total monthly bill? $137, which is a whopping $40 over the price that included the now-cancelled protection plan, and that’s not even accounting for my alleged $30 internet credit. This is the bill for a single line, home internet, and a tablet I can’t even use. There was no internet credit, no special offers. Not. A. Single. Thing. Was. True.

So I asked the agent if I could just cancel everything and return it all, since I’m still within 30 days of purchase, and she informed me that the return period had expired two days ago.

So a dozen agents consistently lied to me for 27 days, and the moment it’s too late to return anything, they drop the hammer and tell the truth. This is no coincidence. T-Mobile is a planned and carefully orchestrated criminal enterprise. I promise the moment my contract is up, I will never do business with them again.

I'm a T-Mobile account holder, I have a cell phone plan that I pay faithfully monthly. I decided to add home internet for Work to start working from home.
T-Mobile representatives have been a disgrace and incompetent at the highest level. I never received my device nor was it rerouted to me , instead I called after 3 weeks and was informed that I wouldn't receive the product and to look for something else. Way to Go T-Mobile!!
The news policy look I am living in Chinese comunist country

Tmobile received the reimbursement from Assurant (the insurance that we pay for and that Tmobile contracts with when we damage our device) but still needed to take the funds from my bank account stating"it takes 1 to 2 billing cycles for the credit to go on your account".  They need my $378 "floating" in their bank for 2 months.   I understand they don't need my money or my account.   I would like to have my $378 dollars in my account.   Imagine the interest they earn on ALL OF THEIR CLIENTS FLOATING FUNDS.   The corporate greed is so disgusting.  Not to mention It has taken 2 calls (LONG CALLS) to get no results and I imagine I will need to call again in "1 to 2 billings cycles".  LOL  All of these companies are a joke.    I laughed hysterically with the customer service rep.  The reps are amazing by the way.  I don't even know how they can do their jobs.  It has to be so difficult for them to be front facing for such disgusting policies.    Thank you!

I received new phone through insurance.  Returned old phone. It was received,  but T-Mobile's warehouse can't find it. They are charging me for the phone. I've been trying since March to get this resolved and haven't been successful.
The company have good deals and they help with almost everything

The representatives I spoke to demonstrated a lack of knowledge and expertise in addressing my concerns. And the so called manager also dammy as well. They both do not know how to do a simple mathematics. They talk over you while you try to explain. They seemed ill-equipped to provide accurate information or effectively resolve my issues. I encountered conflicting answers and inconsistent advice, which only added to my confusion and frustration. It is disheartening to receive such subpar assistance when seeking help and guidance as a valued customer.

Moreover, the lack of empathy and understanding displayed by your representatives was deeply disappointing. I expected a higher level of professionalism and genuine concern for customer satisfaction. Instead, I encountered dismissive attitudes, apathy towards my concerns, and an overall lack of willingness to go above and beyond to assist me. This kind of treatment is simply unacceptable and does not align with the customer-centric values that T-Mobile claims to uphold.

Prepaid is great. Not a problems to be seen. Post paid is a joke. They have such predatory practices. They make it, so if your a few days late, they bump up the bill a bit. They then continue to increase the owed amount bit by bit. They then lock you out of your account, so the debt spirals, you need to visit a local store to verify your identity, and get back into the account. By then your screwed and owe them 3-5 times your normal bill and they won't do anything about it. Do not let these loan sharks eat your credit. You will regret it. Post paid is worse than a scam.

I had the BEST experience with T-Mobile!!!! My mother recently joined and I wasn’t convinced to follow until I talked with customer service representative from Maine, Galin. I was barely interested in joining and was not planning on buying a new phone or joining T-Mobile until my mother called and asked about the services and phones. Galin was so happy and enthusiastic to help us with this and walk us through pricing and plans. He was so so funny and so very helpful. I’ve never had a phone service be so informative. I had the best customer service experience and I hope I can always get help like this again. If not, get help from Galin again!! Thank you for hiring such an amazing spirit! I really hope this reaches T-Mobile, we’re doing the other survey as well because he was just that good!! Thank you Galin!!

It's been absolutely terrible.
I have been in tears numerous times being told several different things by several different people. Trying to get a phone fixed? Starting from the beginning of May, it is now July, and the phone is still not. I have been paying insurance on the phone I am still paying for the phone and nobody has been honest with me one person says don't worry it's taken care of the other says nothing was done then we will get back to you within 72 hours mind you this phone is not a year old. Can't get a straight answer been trying. I am so done I think it's time to go back to AT&T or Verizon. 😢

Been a customer for 15 yrs, gradually gotten worse.
Im an aid worker so always was payg, [mostly never using what I pay for as its a leave in 24hr thing] so on returning would top up. This time paid $67, 2 days later they want more so I called. Was told dont worry you will get a month, day later told would be switched off. I called, again told not to worry would get a month, after an hr on the phone. Next day told fees due and was turned off. Utter shambles, and bare face lied to..after 15 yrs of being a loyal customer. Stay away, they will promise the earth knowing its a lie

A Tower has been down for 4 days. Customers are compressed onto other towers causing massive congestion.
My download speed is as low as 0.8 at times. No Internet, no TV.
T-Mobile says that they have no idea when service will resume. They also refuse to send update messages to folk.

I am a refugee from Europe. I came to the store for a phone, with a minimum knowledge of the language in the city of Yuba City, I changed my old one to a new phone. For the old one, they promised a payment of $ 850. As a result, T-Mobile deceived me very harshly, put me on an exchange program that repays the tariff for 24 months, signed up for a tariff of $ 90 per month, plus signed for insurance in the amount of $ 30. Plus, they charged $666 from my card as the first payment. As a result, I paid more than I needed, and the money that was left allegedly went on credit for future payments. As a result, I gave a loan, and T-Mobile gave me. This is pure deception. I highly recommend T-Mobile. Run from here. At the first opportunity and payment of the loan, I will change the operator forever.

Be careful with T-Mobile.  They offered a phone deal as long as I agreed to the autopay option.  They could not get the phones working (after 4 days) so I canceled and stayed with ATT.  They did not turn off the autopay when I canceled and I did not notice it in my bank account for 6 months. I assumed that canceling the account would also cancel the autopay but they stated it was my responsibility to do it (through them) but separately.  They can only refund 2 months back so I'm out the remaining $700+.  Don't assume they will do their part to cancel the account. As an added note, I also tried to use their internet service and it worked great for about a month.  It was also a great deal for $50 a month but the speed was a joke.  It's true that you get what you pay for. Live and learn.

We went to t-mobile to buy home internet for our cabin in KY.    We have a 800+ credit rating.  We are financially set and have 3 homes paid for.  Yet the customer service (from India) refused to sell us a tower.   The worst part of the process was that after we gave all of our personally information, the next day our credit monitoring company and our bank informed us that our private information was found on the dark web.  If I could give t-mobile a zero rating I would.

T-Mobile is the worst mobile company. Costumer support is terrible I’ve been eating dor a refound for over a month ( it was supposed to pe paid between 7-10 days) I have to keep calling and the never solve my problem.

We had a great experience switching from Verizon to T-mobile at the North Ridgeville Ohio store on Center Ridge Road. Chris was our salesman and was very knowledgeable and helpful. We were given a lot of the promotions and discounts going on. The manager Brian was also very helpful and friendly     We also got the internet special and love it. It works great in our home on all of our tvs, computers, tablets etc. I would recommend them highly. They made the switch of 3 phone lines very easy.

I was initially interested in the Essentials 55 plan for $40/month with auto bill pay. The sales representative showed me, in writing, how I could get many more features, plus a free phone, for $55/month (via monthly reduction of the cost of the phone for 24 months), with the Go5G 55 plan. After signing up for the more costly plan, I learned that some of the features I had been promised were not included in that plan; they were only available on the Go5G Plus 55 plan, which would cost $70/month. I went back to the retail store three times, spoke with the sales rep and the store manager, who both assured me they would "fix" the problem. I later found out they had changed my plan to Go5G Plus 55 without my permission. I than phoned T-Mobile and spoke with two individuals (one being a manager) and was told the only thing they could do for me was to switch me back to the Go5G 55 plan, without the previously promised features. I have not been using T-Mobile long enough to comment on the wireless service.

My name is Reynaldo Yanez I am a senior citizens and I live on a fix income! I have a debt Visa card from Humana it has $51.10 on it! So went to the store yesterday they could process the amount because they wanted to process the $62.35! I told them I just had this amount! So I call Humana and they already paid them $51.10! And I got the transfer ID to prove it talk to them this morning saying they didn't get it bunch of bull! So now going today just going to people the remending balance! Do not do services with T- Mobile they bunch of theifs yes I said it back it up!
The sales rep flat out lied to me about which services were included in my plan and how much it would cost me! I am very angry and disappointed! I would never recommend T Mobile to anyone!

T-Mobile sucks. You can not get service. At times the phone goes straight to voicemail then you can not send messages. It will also say emergency calls only and no text messages at this time. It will give messages like no network found even though there is unlimited data. You can be outside and boom call drops. T-Mobile is horrible. The worst phone company out there.

I got a T-Mobile data plan, no contract, cancel anytime. The offer included a free tablet (a cheap Alcatel one) which I didn't need. The sales rep did NOT explain that the tablet IS on a contract (24 months) and cancelling the service entails paying the remaining "balance" on the "free" tablet. Although I can return the unused tablet, they will not budge. They also declined to check the recording (from 7 months ago) to confirm that there was no mention of this contract. Either sneaky by design or they need to train their customer reps better. I sure hope someone will take them to court for this type of misrepresentations.

Enjoyed my phone and plan. Spent $18 a month for 3.5 years for the protection 360 plan. Decided to try and use it since my phone is falling apart and there’s still a large deductible. Loyalty and being a good customer isn’t rewarded. There aren’t any decent offers. Service is weak and all around my experience has been disappointing. I will be disconnecting.

I was with Sprint for more than a decade before the merger. During that time, my bill for unlimited service was low, customer service was decent by phone and good at our local Sprint store, and my coverage was good. I *did* have some trouble with the 4G internet if I did not have access to Wi-Fi, but when camping in the SE USA or in the mountains of NC, I was often the only person in my group to have cell coverage. 

After the merger... yikes. 

Well, it didn't start well. After being told by mail a number of times that I had to put the enclosed T-Mobile SIM card into my phone to continue serve, I finally bought the lie around the 3rd SIM that they sent. 

I called customer support, as my local Sprint store had already been a casualty of the merger. I wanted clarification if I could add the SIM card without it wiping my phone. After being assured that nothing would happen besides the continuation of my service, I listen to T-Mobile customer service. 

That was a catastrophic error. They were, of course, mistaken. Literally everything on my phone was wiped. Most of it was backed up so it was merely inconvenient They were however a few things that were irreplaceable, which is beyond frustrating even the fact that I had tried to clarify whether or not something like this was possible.

As we have moved forward with T-Mobile, I can say that most of the experiences for my family of six — all of whom are & were always on the same network — have been a lot like that first one. 

T-Mobile customer service employees seem to *want* to be helpful, but they also don't ever actually know what they're doing. 

My bill continues to creep higher, and my service actually continues to get worse! It is ridiculous. 

REGARDLESS of location (indoors/ outdoors, urban/rural, Orlando, Jax, Miami, Atlanta, Asheville, and all points in-between) for all 6 family members, going about our days in various places, to be completely *UNABLE* to: 

• place phone calls (hit send, and screen changes to indicate call is being made, but nothing — no dial tone, ringing, "we're sorry, your call could not be completed, etc. — happens. After about a minute, it just fully disconnects)

• send texts (with or without attachments)

• receive calls/texts from others (It is not unusual for all of us to suddenly, but often not at the same time, receive a dozen or more notifications regarding voicemails and texts that have just arrived but are often days old)

We now all have Google voice numbers that we use for over Wi-Fi calling for business & important calls because that FREE service is more reliable than our T-Mobile service. It is absolutely maddening.

If I could give 0 stars I would!! T-Mobile is full of lies!! I was promised a bill of $158.09 to switch to their new Go5gPlus. They are full of lies. My bill was $257.90 when I contacted support they said that they can't do anything due to the promotions they have given me. They refuse to help.or stand by their word! I contacted them 6/23/2023 and 6/30/2023 with no resolutions. I had the agent on 6/23 tell me they would call back in 24 to 48 hours. It's now been over 7 days!. Don't get T-Mobile!!

The headline says it all. I ordered the 5g service in the middle of the night to use as a backup for recent outages by Spectrum. I do not use T Mobile for my phones so they have to assign you a “phone number” for your router/modem to connect. I received the modem in a couple of days and mounted it in a high cabinet by a front window of the house. Had an adequate signal and turned on the televisions to see if they worked. The reception came and went over two days, not a reliable reception to pay 50 bucks/month. Packed it all up and returned it on day two…if you don’t return after your trial you will be charged. I returned this in February and in March they send me a credit on my account of $43.00 ….what am I to do with a credit on a T Mobile account ??? I don’t use them for phones or internet. I have contacted them numerous times to please refund my money but first they want me to give them a a PIN that I don’t remember. Why? I gave them the account number they had a credit card number why not issue a refund on the card? I did go to a T Mobile store to see if they could resolve this mess. The representative that helped me, spent half an hour on the phone ( with their customer service) and then after they got my bank account number etc. they gave me their word it was done. It is now coming up on July fourth weekend and two weeks later…still no money returned.

When I say I dislike this provider and they’re customer service representatives. I have been a Sprint customer since 2013 and ever since they merged with T mobile it’s been horrible. My service sucks and every time you call you have to wait at least 45 minutes. There has got to be something better. I have needed to upgrade my phone since May but because of the updates I can’t get a new phone until they complete the update they are doing.

T-Mobile is not honest about billing. They donot tell you the correct billing amount at the time of order. You get a shock when you receive your first bill. When you complain about it, the customer service has the pet reply, "This is our company policy and we cannot do anything about it." Avoid this service provider, the worst experience I had dealing with this company.
Switching to a Christian cell phone service because I'm tired of paying for "everyone is racist and communist and sex isn't private" policies (DEI, ESG, neo-Pride...).

This is difficult, because I had a wonderful experience with the Bellingham WA call center. But I started in the Shoreline Midvale and Aurora physical store and have never been treated more rudely. So I would review the call center in WA 5+ and the physical store 0.  I am not sure why the sales rep was so rude but she needs to find a new job is she is that unhappy. Also she flat out lied when she said that I had to call customer service to resolve my problem. As the call center apologized and said they could have easily resolved your issue in the store.
Service sucks and constantly drops.

Paid for insurance on my phone and now that my screen is broke I get nothing but run-a-rounds, I came to the store for help, and the manager here has been trying too, for over an hour now.   This is ridiculous!

One of the worst experiences of my life.  I was convinced to switch to T-Mobile from Verizon.  But the sales rep who I chatted with didn't include the 20% discount and free Pixel 7a phone on the account so my bill is way higher than it should be.  But that's not the worst part so far.  I have spent 7 hours on the phone getting jerked around from one department to the next because my number port from Verizon didn't work properly.  I cannot receive calls.  I can only make calls.  No one can help.  I am stuck with no phone and a bill that is way higher than promised.  I've never felt so ripped off in my life.  Genuinely one of the worst decisions I've made as a consumer in decades to try and save $40 a month.

Horrible company to have as a Cell phone provider. They wont release your number once requested over and over. They steal from their clients and play stupid when you call them on it always blaming the rep who helped you earlier. All they try to do is tie you to contract and when you refuse to fall or that they make it impossible to leave. All i wanted was a transfer pin which should have been the pin i had provided them with. But they change it in order to keep you against your will. Horrible is only the start, save yourself the aggravation.

Absolute trash. Pay for 5g.  Get 4g that runs slower than dialup.  T-mobile has a reputation of screwing people with low priority service quality if you bad talk them and are not a cellebrity / political figure head.  Thx to all the intellectually superior people who voted against net nutrality.  Enjoy those price hikes poor service and throttled bandwidth.

Be very careful when purchasing this accessory - ask LOTS of questions.  We got 2 as part of a "free promotion" on April 19th at the Frisco TX T-Mobile store.  It wasn't clearly explained about the extra $5 per month to connect the device to each phone line.  Plus, the devices were simply problematic and didn't work for us on our European trip.  But they won't take them back, even though they're defective.  Customer Service did offer a one time credit for ONE of the devices, which is appreciated.  The bigger problem is the lack of transparency about what this "free" item will ultimately cost you.   It was not explained clearly in store and I can not see anything about the costs on the paperwork I have.   When returning to the store after our trip, they said call customer service since it was past 30 days.  Of course, customer service said we should have returned it to the store.  !?   
The two associates I spoke with on the phone were professional and helpful.  But I place the blame squarely on T-Mobile leadership who probably don't provide adequate training or support for the stores.

Was a new customer was promised they will pay off my phone which was under $500. Took a month never see that $500 call them back and was told if I trade in my phone they will help pay off and give me $800 for trade in. on top of that, instead of $800 towards my phone I only got $350 which they put that towards my billing so I never saw the $450. Got mad waited til my billing cycle to end with that gay ass $350. Paid off my phone and told them I am leaving them as a new customer. They try to give me 10 bucks off every month I was like screw that. I regret leaving my over cell provider, paid off my phone and went back to my old provider. If they can lie to you once, they can keep on lying because that’s exactly what they did to me for every phone called I had to deal with them. I call this company more than I call my wife!!!!

I have had a T-Mobile phone for two years and the 5G is garbage. No bars! I have called customer service several times to see if they can fix it and after an hour each time on the phone, they do nothing to improve my service. Anywhere I go in Northwest Indiana there is bad reception. My advice to anyone thinking of getting T-Mobile is not to. It is garbage. Their customer service is garbage. My advice to T-Mobile is to stop with all the extra crap you promote and concentrate on the phone service. The backbone of your company is the reception! Work on it!!! You are garbage T-Mobile!  Garbage!
Went to the TMobile store in Woodstock VA and was essentially ignored by store personnel for over 20 minutes. I ended up having to travel 40 miles to Harrisonburg to get attended.

I went to the T-Mobile store in Hyannis, MA. My cell phone died 3 weeks before and I was caught in the middle between the insurance company and the repair facility (who ended up closing their doors). When my claim was approved, someone suggested we say goodbye to our current carrier and go with T-Mobile. We're so glad we did. Michael is great to deal with. He thoroughly explained our options. When we decided to go with T-Mobile he spent time with us to set up our phones. Since our initial dealings with Michael, we've been back to the store for a couple of things we had a hard time figuring out and the staff that helped us was great. We really appreciate the staff in the Hyannis location and highly recommend them. Thank you to Michael and the team!

I moved to T-mobile from other network based on the promises given by T-Mobile, I paid all the termination fee and paid off phones from pocket to T-mobile for high speed data, I experienced 1Mbps under the topest plan of T-mobile (Go5G Plus), I reached to Customer Service, they were not ready to find resolution or compensation. All they said is you can leave T-mobile, nothing i can do in harsh tone. Not a customer centric at all.

I will never buy a single phone accessory from the Metro by T-Mobile store @2465 Nazareth Road in Easton, PA ever again. Their sales people sell phone chargers not even compatible with your iPhone. After just 2 months, the charger will tell you it’s incompatible & won’t work. Meanwhile, you only get 14 days to return something! It makes no sense to spend $25 on a charger from them when you can get one so cheap on Amazon & theirs last way longer. Good bye Metro by T-Mobile!
The costumer service is bad and they erased EVERYTHING on my phone

Not disability friendly. Customer service I understand has their hands tied, but the main company can do better. The fact i have all my information to prove I am me for a CELL SERVICE not a bank but a simple cell company but can not open my account up unless i go physically to a store is ridiculous and not disability friendly. Not everyone is capable of driving and getting to a store to prove they are them, when i have my social, address, account number, phone number, payment amounts, email, and log in to prove I am me. Then there response is they will charge you 10$ to pay your bill via the phone. Very disappointed with such a big company that is not disability friendly.

I will never get a phone from T-Mobile…
I will never get a phone from T-Mobile and I would never give them my card because they screwed me out of my money $154 which I did not give them permission to take that out of my account. I asked him for a iPhone they sent me a 11 phone which I did not want are use for two months, three months or more they would not give me the phone that I asked for they have lied and stolen money from my account. They will sit there and lie to you through their teeth and make you wait and wait on the phone for other people that is not going to help you, I would never buy a phone from T-Mobile. I hope people read this text and I hope they do not give their card up for a lousy five dollars to say they will do not buy from T-Mobile. I repeat, do not buy from T-Mobile. They will screw you.
Great service right to the point Jose was very helpfull!!!

Ive been a Tmobile customer for years.  I just transferred one of my phone to a company plan as I started at a new company and they are paying for my phone.  When I transferred, I will still getting billed for 2 phones.  I called twice and was on the phone with customer service for over an hour and they could not explain why I am still being billed for 2 lines months after I changed my phone to company plan.  went into the tmobile store and all we received is no explanation at all.  since i've been paying for 2 phones since January, made hours of phone calls, and went into the Seekonk, MA store to get nothing changed - and the manager I spoke to was very rude. time for a new cell phone company!!! ridiculous!

TMobile is the worst service and a scam used there insurance to get my replacement phone and they sent me a faulty on then when asking for a replacement for that they said they were out of stock and I'd have to wait a month for the phone they chose to send which is a cheap dime a dozen phone they are a scam and liar and customer support was beyond rude and unhelpful.

Ordered internet. They sent to wrong address. We tracked it down and drove it to our T-Mobile store. She asked what happened and stated before I said one full sentence. "I don't care" and flippantly walked away. 
I guess t mobile doesn't care. Lol  what a mess!
Many places I get no service, poor service, or slow at best. 5G is a joke. Should have never left Verizon!!

I will never get a phone from T-Mobile and I would never give them my card because they screwed me out of my money $154 which I did not give them permission to take that out of my account. I asked him for a iPhone they sent me a 11 phone which I did not want are use for two months, three months or more they would not give me the phone that I asked for they have lied and stolen money from my account. They will sit there and lie to you through their teeth and make you wait and wait on the phone for other people that is not going to help you, I would never buy a phone from T-Mobile. I hope people read this text and I hope they do not give their card up for a lousy five dollars to say they will do not buy from T-Mobile. I repeat, do not buy from T-Mobile. They will screw you.

T-Mobile Travel has horrible service. Stayed at a hotel that wasn't ready to check in at 3pm, and room wasn't cleaned until 10pm. And it smelled like cigarettes. Brought to t mobile concierge and they did nothing.

If could give T-mobile 0 points I would I have never worked qith such a rubbish company I paid for a service I didn't receive I was promised a supervisor called and promised a refund which I never received when I called back I was told it was scheduled and I should receive it in five days I never got my refund nor my service. Don't use them it's a total scam TJEY JUST STEAL PEOPLE'S  MONEY!!
WORST Customer Service EVER! They lie through their teeth to sign you up, then don't do anything they say. I've wasted hours on chats and calls, and things only get worse

I have 3 unresolved issues t-m has never fixed.  So called " experts" don't have a clue.  Today McAfee suddenly raised from the dead.  Called, no one knows why, then supervisor comes on telling me I need it because of my "NEW" phone (6 months old).  I tell her that is CRAP.  McAfee looks at programs not physical devices.  Also since they ask me 100 questions for verifying MYSELF to them, I want to know WHO authorized this charge.  No comment given other than "we are escalating this matter", which I am still waiting for the last 3 escalations.

They are constantly changing things for the worse. Plan prices have gone up across-the-board. Now your forced to use a debit card or bank account draft to receive the 5$ autopay discount they've advertised. Use a credit card and your bill goes up again

this is a company of scammers at the beginning they give you a free phone, then they start taking money for this phone, then when you come in and redeem it, I will pay the full price for it, they will not unlock it and start promising you that they will do it just now, they have a whole team of stress  -managers who listen to all your suggestions and supposedly help you reassure and say that they will definitely help you that something is sure to happen to you just about good, but as a result of neither money nor a learned phone, they constantly keep the phone impossible to use it by other companies and are going to use it  full cost

The T-Mobile company and the service itself is a total joke. I keep getting messages that say we're working on the tower that you use most to improve your experience but it has gotten even worse than it was. Customer service is a joke!  She couldn't help me with a simple question..... how much do I owe.  I ended the call 57 minutes after finally getting the answer I was asking. I would give them 0 if that was an option.  The mobile service is poor/slow on new phones. The customer service is terrible for simple questions.

T-Mobile is garbage company .
They want same  money like competitor for bad services.
I don’t even try next time if they give free services

When I signed up for cell service and internet, she pitched the sync up drive, which can give you a hotspot in your car.  I was told that the service is $10 a month and that I can cancel anytime.  After receiving the device I found out that it is not compatible with my car, so I call T-Mobile to cancel it.  I was then told that if I cancel I will be charged over $100 for the crappy little device.  No option to return it.  T-Mobile only cares about profit, they do not care about customer service and loyalty.  I will be cancelling my phone and internet service as soon as I decide on the company that deserves my business.

22 years of being a loyal customer asked for a free sweatshirt.  Also free advertisement for them. They instead gave me a free watch because they said they can do better than a sweatshirt.  After I recieved the watch I. Saw taxes for the watch on my bill which is higher then before. I called and they are charging me $13.70 first 24 months. That $330.00!!! What was free??? That's the price of the watch!!! And now I'm in a contract that I have not been in for 8 years!! All my devices I paid cash for.  NOW T-Mobile is saying I have to return the "gift" they gave me or pay $330.00
WOW!!
I switched to t mobile and they promised me that my bill won’t change  now they keep raising my bill every month be aware they are scammers take your business somewhere else

The service is horrible! I am in an area where we cannot get unlimited Internet, therefore each month we hit our limit. We are unable to stream services or work from home! DO NOT go with T-Mobile! I did get help with being charged for items that were “free” when I signed up and didn’t want the items in the first place! The biggest problem is that I cannot make a call from my home if I have Wi-Fi on. I have to turn it off on my phone to make a call! That is crazy! I am stuck with T-Mobile for a year. I am moving the second it is over and May even pay a penalty to get out early!

I was with Verizon for 12 years, due to big incentives and rebates, we moved to T Mobile.
I was told and written down that $800/iphone and $1000 for Samsung trade in rebates.
Today was 3rd call for rebates and trade in credit. 
While on phone, they noticed I had given negative ratings.  She bluntly told me I need to take my issues to the store.  She denied rebates.

With Verizon:
I never had billing issues with Verizon.
I never had customer service attitude problems With Verizon.

I was with Sprint since 1995 (28 years). I never had an issue and when something minor came up, it was always resolved quickly, easily, and most importantly, with care.

Sprint was taken over by T-Mobile. I migrated less than a year ago and this is one of the worst companies I have ever come into contact with. T-Mobile does not care about its customers and worse, lies. I was treated like I didn’t matter by the assistant manager of this location.

I have not been able to make or receive calls from my home since Sunday morning. I have called (from my computer and wi-fi) T-Mobile countless times (I stopped counting at 20), finding no resolution. I was told about ‘possible’ tower issues, given the runaround, and directions that never fixed the issue. Finally, they admitted the tower was having issues and my phone was not 5G so it would not work.

After not being able to make or receive calls for 4 days, they said they would give me a new phone with 5G with the hopes that this would resolve the issue. They admitted the issue was on their end and I should not have to pay for a new phone. I finally felt I had a resolution to an extremely frustrating, aggravating, and upsetting 4 days.

Everything was set, I was told to go to my local T-Mobile store, and everything would be ready. When I got there, I was told, "Sorry, that promise is not in the system and there is nothing I can do for you." I asked to speak to the manager and was told, "He is in a meeting and can't do anything for you as well."

I asked the assistant manager to call T-Mobile for me to clear the issue. She said she would not but I could call. I explained that my phone could not make calls out because I am still near the tower with issues, and she said I could use their landline. Can you imagine?!! A T-Mobile who was in the T-Mobile store could not call T-Mobile to help me resolve the issue for a T-Mobile customer. However, the customer could call T-Mobile from the T-Mobile store's phone. Have you ever heard of such nonsense and disregard for a customer?

In the store, I called customer service (AGAIN). After spending AN HOUR on the phone, being transferred from person to person, and having to explain the issue over and over again, finally, a rep said, "Yes, I can see what Luna put on your account. You are supposed to get a new phone from us because the issue is with us. However, it was not authorized, and I will need to speak to Luna to get one last code. Can you hold again?" Another 30 MINUTES later, after the rep talked to the assistant manager, and thinking it was resolved, I got back on the phone and was told, "I am still trying to get a hold of Luna." I finally gave up, hung up, and left the store. 

I was given the runaround, lied to, then treated like I didn’t matter by a T-Mobile assistant manager. I am not with another carrier and will never recommend T-Mobile to anyone. I will file complaints anywhere I can as this experience was horrible and people should know.

What happens to, “We are so sorry you are having major issues but this will be resolved before you walk out the door. Give me a little time to investigate and find a resolution.” Instead, I received, “It is our fault and there is nothing we can or will do to help you.”

I miss you Sprint. You were the best company I had the privilege of being a customer with for 28 years.

I purchased the T-mobile tower for internet from Morris, 
IL. I was told I would get a bill via email for my invoice on the first or 2nd of June, I did not receive so I called on 5/31 and was told give it a few days, after the weekend I had a past due email, I tried to contact several people who, for a week and a half just transferred me back and forth with no one helping, then I was told I had to come to the store, which I didn't have time for. They cut off my service and now I had to pay $140.00 including a reactivation fee. In the mean time I talked to Elaina from the Plainfield store, I was happy to meet someone who new what they were talking about, one customer service guy kept calling me Linda, I finally called him Karen and told him my name is Maureen. The customer service I have received throughout this process has been unacceptable and cost me more $ then it was worth. Anyone planning on buying anything T-mobile, I recommend Elaina from Plainfield, Never Morris. This experience started on 5/31/23 and is not over yet on 6/13/23

Needed to upgrade a SIM card.  TMobile store said just take it home, pop it in and punch in the SIM card and you'll be good.  Ha Ha   Sounded easy.  30 minutes later on hold with customer service still.  Is anyone working these phones?

The other reviews are TRUE. These guys are a SCAM! Never... ever... ever sign up for T-Mobile. 

Promised rebates are not being honored. Service is horrible... the bill keeps getting raised higher and higher. Phones are not working and they do not resolve the issue. They with-hold your trade-in values and give them as 24 installments... but they never actually get made. I feel like a fool for not looking at the hundred of thousands of BAD REVIEWS on the internet.  If I can help just ONE other person avoid this absolute disaster of a company... I will have done a good deed. 

1.) The incentives are always deferred and are oftewn never paid. 

2.) The advertisements are a "bait and switch" - they are misleading and disingenuine.

3.) The customer service is consistently over-seas and suffers from difficutly in communication. 

4.) The service flat out does not work at times - without any real excuse. (My text messages consistently do not get delivered)

T mobile continued to bill me months after my account was closed stating a third unused complimentary line was left open and to they continued to bill me. The complimentary line was unknown, unused, and assigned without my knowledge. Criminal. FCC contacted.

We switched last month with a boat load of promised rebates and discounts.  Almost nothing has been honored.  Customer service would not address my complaints.  I had to take my concerns to the Florida Attorney General, Florida Department of Consumer Services and the Federal Trade Commission to get my problems addressed.  So glad the conversation was via their website text and I retained a copy of the transcript.

Still a long way from getting resolved and getting all the promised rebates and discounts.  And the amount of time I've put into this is ridiculous.  BUYER BEWARE!!!

Worst company ever. Thieves.
They have constantly been increasing my bill since 4 months and I still don't have a resolution after multiple hours with customer care. They keep making me run around in circles and I have to explain everything from the start. I wish I never took the connection. Hate this company.
Why are they doing pride month? I never seen straight month. Always dropping service.  Certain areas don't get service . Told it would be a Certain amount each month that was wrong .
Horrible phone internet for 2 years.  They drug out us closing our account so they could bill us another month!  Crooked business practices!

T-MOBILE ARE SCAMMERS. HOW THEY ARE STILL IN BUSINESS IS BEYOND ME. AVOID THESE CLOWNS!

Nine weeks to cancel my deceased father‘s account, because I didn’t know his pin number. Who is supposed to know your pin number other than yourself??! I am on his account because he had dementia and that is still not enough to simply bring a death certificate in and be done. I have been given the runaround, for seven weeks, still being charged, as if my father is alive, and of course, now his account is suspended and delinquent. None of the other accounts in my fathers life, which are much more serious than a telephone, had an issue with excepting my word or the death certificate. T-Mobile has the most convoluted systems in their billing and accounts services that I will forever advertise my experience and the inability for T-Mobile to provide any sort of customer service. I have to wait two weeks at a specific time for their phone call to hopefully get this canceled. We’ve already tried this once before and nobody called. There’s something terribly wrong with this company and I would suggest that everyone look elsewhere!

If I could give T Mobile  zero stars I would.  This is the most ridiculous cell phone company...I have ever dealt with.  I would NEVER recommend them.  HORRIBLE customer service, does nothing to help with issue, no call backs as said they would, no compensation to long time loyal customers when TMOBILE screws up you account.....DO NOT USE T MOBILE.

Customer service customer service you say what is customer service?. It's a series of passing you from here to there having a press 1 press 2 press 4 and if you don't do it fast enough to like thank. You hang up. Then if you get somebody you pass around multiple times to the same departments. It took me over a day and a half to actually activate a new phone. Manufacturer issue. I'll settle down I'm using a pretty patient woman. I ended up hanging up on multiple people that I was on the phone for a good hour with because they kept going to surfing getting nowhere. And talk about scripts. With short knee scripts and not ask the same questions for what dmm times in a row. If I can find a company to buy out my contract I will do that. And these call centers probably have thousands of people shoulder to shoulder to shoulder each one of them if have to chat To  5 chats customers at a time. Ninety percent of my conversations were unintelligible due to language barrier. Oh and if you have a bad connection don't expect to call back because they're not allowed to do that. If you have a broken phone and your numbers don't work forget about it because you can't get anywhere without pressing a number there's no way to. Just talk to a human no way at all.. That one star I feel extremely generous.

"Beware of their scamming sales campaigns, that if you buy a phone from their stores, it will get unlocked after 3 months! scam!"

They promised a trial of their 5G Home Internet. Didn't work, extremely slow, returned it quickly. Promised for three months to refund the money, never did what they promised. Now I'm disputing the credit card charge. I will never use T Mobile for anything.

I have been a tmobile customer 22 years. It used to be good. Lately it's awful. It takes very very long time to get tech support. My galaxy s22 has been problem for months noone there can give me an answer.

What can I say? Been a customer for at least 5 years… day I get arrested, I find out T-Mobile helped the police by deleting specific txt/call logs from my account that could've proved my innocence! Giving the police any info they need is one thing but assisting with DELETING info is pretty shady, don't you think? When I called and confronted the issue,I was being connected to several different people cause no one wanted to address my concerns or maybe they just didn't know how? Only response that was provided to me was to contact Apple to get records. Well Apple doesn't have any control over my actual T-MOBILE PHONE RECORDS…but thx for all the help. Wow…. Either way, they're completely untrustworthy and very misleading. I will be switching to Verizon. I just don't know how you can get away with that or let the police coax you somehow into doing this favor for them. That was my innocence you took from me.

I'm not mad at all… just thought they would look out for me more than that.

I switched my internet from Xfinity (which is a special customer service hell of its own) to T-Mobile. Made the mistake of signing up for autopay. Omg. They just drafted an extra $236 out of my account. The usual charge is $55. I'm on the phone with billing and they are telling me it is a "non-returned" equipment charge. What? I have no non-returned equipment. Do not give this company your billing info. They are not ethical and customer service is clueless.
This company hàs poor promotion guidelines and qualifications.  I'm disgusted already and will be thankful when my contract expires so I can go elsewhere.

Never would I recommend this company to my worst enemy. I have never had such a poor retail experience in my life with any business such as this. They lied to me from the very start saying that I could transfer my government low income discount from Metro to T-Mobile that all I had to do is call their customer support 611and give them my government ID number which I tried to do the first day only to be told that they do not honor the government discount program that it was only for prepaid services I took all the equipment back and was told that I would be refunded and not charged.another lie. Then they wanted to charge me a restocking fee for the equipment $150 for each item that I had just purchased that day.There is never a manager in the stores to speak with and when there is it's never the same manager that was last in the store, high turnover rate amongst the employees and managers. Very poor customer service and long wait times. Called their customer service division only be told after waiting 2 hours that they could not help me because I had no PIN number for the account, I was never given one. I asked repeatedly for the district manager's name and phone number only to be told that they didn't have one which I knew was another lie. Finally I was given a bogus name of Kevin and told to call The number that they gave me which was simply a customer service number and not a district supervisors number. Lie after lie managers in the store say they're not authorized to credit any accounts it had to be done through customer service yet customer service tells you that you have to return to the store. Six times I went to the same store to try and get a manager to help me only to be told they were on vacation or the managers had moved. Then I was told to go to a corporate store that they called to prepare them for me, as a corporate store were the only ones that could be of any help. when I got there I was pleasantly greeted and told that the store I'd been sent to was not a corporate store at all and that they couldn't help me. Lie lie lie. A new employee that had only been there a month by the name of Somalia did try to do her best but there was also no manager there either to help and she would have to call her manager and their customer service division. Customer service said that they could reset a PIN number for me in order to get into the account they saw that I had closed the account and returned all the equipment however I was receiving a bill from a collection agency for 350 dollars. I never use their phones that I purchased and my lines were never ported over to T-Mobile from Metro. I was never told about any service fees for porting my numbers over and their customer service refused to credit My non-existing account and that they still had to charge me for the parting service of three lines. I don't know how this company stays in business and am appalled at their lack of ethics.
A person could use two cans and a string and have better phone service than what this company offers. Please save yourself a lot of time and aggravation and being lied to time and time again and telling you what they think you want to hear in order to take your money.I would never recommend T-Mobile to anyone, and will make it my lifetime commitment to warn everyone I know or come in contact with what a scam T-Mobile is. I feel sorry for anyone that works there or anyone that has anything to do with this company. If I could give them a negative star rating I would.

Lawrenceburg, TN

This is the absolute best customer service from t mobile I've had in 15 years. Literally the only positive experience I've had in that time. Thank you to Trevor and Alex. You need something from t mobile in TN this is the spot to go to no cap

I worked with Meli at T-Mobile customer care support and must say it was the most positive experience I've had through their call center. I generally go into the stores for resolution but in this case, Meli was incredibly patient, empathetic and definitely in troubleshooting mode and determined to get to the bottom of my inquiry. We have NOT always had the greatest customer care experience w/ T-Mobile (over the phone) but if Meli is any indicator, things have certainly improved exponentially! Kudos to her!!!!!!
There can be no worse customer service than Tmobile. DO NOT ISE THEM. THEY WILL ONLY ASK YOU FOR MONEY AND YOU WILL GET BILLED MORE THAN THEY TELL YOU. BUYER BEWARE!!!

Two weeks into a contract after switching from Verizon I got a text message telling me my two lines were suspended. Customer service told me it was a 'voluntary military suspension'. No one could tell me what that was or how it happened. My livelihood depends on a reliable phone line. If this ever happens again I'm gone!

Buyer beware. If I could give -5 stars to T-Mobile, I would. Every single interaction I've had with this company has been a remarkable combination of incompetence, lies, obfuscation, misdirection, and any other antonym to the notion of "service" that you can think of. Stay away or you will regret it.

worst customer service I’ve ever experienced in my life. You do nothing but lie to people and tell them what they wanna hear and then give them the homework to do it. Call 611. T-Mobile by Best Buy in Carlsbad in the forum. Worst Manager ever that man should be fired.
The company does not know how to work in a complex way, registered on the site and could not confirm pin that was just installed

I changed provider from T-mobile to Xfinity. I received a letter stating I owe money. So I called Customer service to find out why. They said I owed for the month of February to April. I started with Xfinity April 10th. So I payed 142.67 on today May 29th. So I called to tell them that it was paid. Them this customer Serivce said I owed an additional 59.00 dollars. I said for what. He said for April to May. I said by that time I was with Xfinity. He check to see if there was any data doing that time and it wasn’t. Now granted when I called the first time the customer service person said it was incorrect they would take it off. I call the second customer service person they had no clue. So now I have to wait 7 days for answer for their screw up.

This is the worst phone service I have ever experienced.  Constant issues with phone connection, text messaging, SIM cards, you name it.  After spending four hours on yet another customer service call, I had enough and switched to another carrier.

Business account services are worse than useless. Not only did they fail to execute the requested transaction, the information they gave us was incorrect. The call center sent us to a company store, saying they were the only ones who could execute a change of responsibility, but the staff at the store told us that that was something that only the call center could do. I had store staff on the line with the call center and the store manager had to instruct the call center staff how to do their job. This is the second time this has happened on our business account. Time to change carriers!
T-Moble has a Catch 22. They won't warranty a phone if the screen has pixelation. But Asurion won't insure the phone unless it's been damaged, exposed to water, lost or stolen.

Tmobile promotes savings but actually creates a bunch of useless “bundles” which are really gimmicks. Having a “free” Netflix subscription or access to Disney+ is actually just priced into your monthly Magenta plans. In terms of saving money you’ll end up losing way more time. Since switching about 7 months ago I’ve spend nearly 12 hours on the phone with customer service for the simplest items. Every call or update that doesn’t add more services takes forever and they always have a “free” promotion which you’ll certainly pay for. With the Magenta Max plans of two lines you automatically get a 3rd line for “free” and was convinced to get a “free” phone which they bill you but credit back for 24 months. They also sneak in a phone protection plan for $10 per month for a Galaxy A14 which is literally a $150 phone that you can get on Amazon. So you have to be careful with all these so called free phones or lines as they are quick to get you on board but upon closing any lines you’ll be responsible for the balance of that free phone which is above market price. Fast forward I got caught for not reading between the line and paid the remaining balance of $209 for a phone they retails at $240 that’s selling for $150. About a month later and having spoken to 4 agents and spent around 4 hours I can’t unlock that phone. On all 4 occasions I was told it would be permanently unlocked within 24-48 hours, never happened. I’ve lost so much time that I actually just gave up and will just gift this phone to someone and just port my number over. Forgot to mention billing, everytime you make changes to your account, watch your billing as they will add a bunch of charges that you’ll have to call to remove next billing cycle. At one point my bill was $290 for two lines and had to call and get credit back. It’s just exhausting and not worth it. PS the hot spot internet is garbage, I’ve had trouble connecting in NYC, Philadelphia, South Jersey literally in major cities, just imagine if you’re in a small town. By far the worst experience I’ve had, I don’t know what happened I had T mobile years ago and didn’t experience this.
